reasoning: Applied the error taxonomy (grammar, vocabulary, pragmatic categories) with severity calibrated against CEFR A1 descriptors. L1 transfer analysis used Spanish-English interlanguage patterns (null subjects, ser/estar-have/be age construction, false friends, adjective placement, preposition mapping). Errors expected at A1 (article confusion, preposition variability) rated minor; errors involving structures that should be emerging at A1+ (3rd person -s, basic question formation) rated moderate to major based on frequency and communicative impact. Self-corrections were tracked as evidence of developing interlanguage awareness.

errors.original: I go to Camden Market
errors.corrected: I went to Camden Market
errors.explanation: Student used present simple instead of past simple when narrating a weekend event. Context clearly required past tense ("how was your weekend").
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - Spanish also requires past tense here ("fui"), so this is a developmental error (incomplete acquisition of irregular past forms) rather than direct transfer

errors.original: Is very nice there!
errors.corrected: It is very nice there!
errors.explanation: Missing dummy subject "it". Spanish allows null subjects ("Es muy bonito alli") but English requires an explicit subject.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish is a pro-drop language; "es" does not require an overt subject. This is direct syntactic transfer from L1.

errors.original: Is very cold today in London!
errors.corrected: It is very cold today in London!
errors.explanation: Same null subject pattern as #2. Weather expressions in Spanish ("Hace frio") do not require a subject.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - pro-drop transfer, reinforced by Spanish weather constructions that use impersonal verbs

errors.original: In Spain is not so cold
errors.corrected: In Spain it is not so cold
errors.explanation: Recurring null subject pattern.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - pro-drop transfer

errors.original: Actually I live in London for three months now
errors.corrected: Currently I live in London for three months now
errors.explanation: Used "actually" (en realidad) when meaning "actualmente" (currently). Classic Spanish-English false friend.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - direct lexical transfer; "actualmente" looks like "actually" but means "currently"

errors.original: I go in the university every day
errors.corrected: I go to the university every day
errors.explanation: Used "in" instead of "to" for direction of movement.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish uses "en" for both location and some directional contexts; overgeneralization of "in" from "en"

errors.original: I have the sister and the brother
errors.corrected: I have a sister and a brother
errors.explanation: Used definite article "the" instead of indefinite "a" when introducing family members for the first time.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish uses definite articles with family ("tengo la hermana") more freely than English

errors.original: She have sixteen years
errors.corrected: She is sixteen years old
errors.explanation: Double error: (a) used "have" instead of "be" for age, and (b) missing 3rd person -s on "have". Spanish expresses age with "tener" (to have): "tiene dieciseis anos."
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - direct structural transfer from "tener + anos" construction

errors.original: Why you don't have years in English?
errors.corrected: Why don't you have years in English?
errors.explanation: Incorrect word order in a wh-question. Subject and auxiliary not inverted.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - Spanish wh-questions can have SVO order ("Por que tu no tienes..."), though inversion is also common

errors.original: My father work in a hospital
errors.corrected: My father works in a hospital
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person singular -s on present simple verb.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - Spanish 3rd person conjugation is more salient (-a/-e endings); English -s is less perceptually salient and easily omitted

errors.original: Is because I practice a lot
errors.corrected: It is because I practice a lot
errors.explanation: Recurring null subject pattern.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - pro-drop

errors.original: He run in the house all day
errors.corrected: He runs in the house all day
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person -s.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - low perceptual salience of English -s

errors.original: My mother don't like cats
errors.corrected: My mother doesn't like cats
errors.explanation: Used "don't" instead of "doesn't" for 3rd person singular negation.

errors.original: she have two cats
errors.corrected: she has two cats
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person form "has" for irregular verb "have."

errors.original: she live in a small village
errors.corrected: she lives in a small village
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person -s.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - low salience of -s

errors.original: I forget always
errors.corrected: I always forget
errors.explanation: Adverb of frequency placed after the verb instead of before it.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish places frequency adverbs after the verb ("olvido siempre")

errors.original: she is very sympathetic with everyone
errors.corrected: she is very friendly with everyone
errors.explanation: Used "sympathetic" (feeling sorry for) when meaning "simpatica" (friendly/nice). Spanish-English false friend.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - direct lexical transfer from "simpatico/a"

errors.original: My mother is a woman very nice
errors.corrected: My mother is a very nice woman
errors.explanation: Post-nominal adjective placement following Spanish word order.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish places adjectives after nouns ("una mujer muy simpática")

errors.original: He always help everyone
errors.corrected: He always helps everyone
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person -s.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial

errors.original: He like to read the newspaper
errors.corrected: He likes to read the newspaper
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person -s.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial

errors.original: He live in his own world
errors.corrected: He lives in his own world
errors.explanation: Missing 3rd person -s.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial

errors.original: In Christmas
errors.corrected: At Christmas
errors.explanation: Incorrect preposition for holidays.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish uses "en Navidad"

errors.original: there is many chairs
errors.corrected: there are many chairs
errors.explanation: Used singular "there is" with plural noun.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish "hay" is invariable for singular and plural

errors.original: clothes in the floor, in the bed
errors.corrected: clothes on the floor, on the bed
errors.explanation: Used "in" instead of "on" for surfaces.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish "en" maps to both "in" and "on"

errors.original: more tall
errors.corrected: taller
errors.explanation: Used analytic comparative instead of synthetic form for short adjective.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish uses analytic comparatives ("mas alto"), direct transfer of structure
